title: Entrepreneurship
objective: Students will explore entrepreneurship concepts including Costs & Revenue, Making a Simple Plan, Learning from Failure and more.

steps.content: Today we're going to learn about Costs & Revenue. Get ready for an adventure!
It costs money to make things (costs/expenses); you earn money by selling them (revenue); if revenue is more than costs, that's profit; if costs are more, that's a loss
Let's practice together! Explain what costs, revenue, and profit mean using a simple example
If you spent £4 on supplies for a lemonade stand and earned £10 selling lemonade, could they tell you how much profit they made?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Costs & Revenue: Explain what costs, revenue, and profit mean using a simple example Calculate profit from a given scenario (e.g. spent £3 on ingredients, sold cakes for £8, profit = £5)
Have you ever thought about Making a Simple Plan? Let's find out together!
Before starting a business, making a simple plan: what will I make or do, who will buy it, what do I need, how much will it cost, and what price will I charge?
Let's practice together! Write or draw a simple business plan covering: product, customer, materials needed, and price
If you wanted to run a small business at a school fair, could they write a simple plan covering what they'd sell, what they'd need, and what they'd charge?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Making a Simple Plan: Write or draw a simple business plan covering: product, customer, materials needed, and price Identify at least three things they would need before they could start selling
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Learning from Failure!
Not every business idea works; entrepreneurs try, fail, learn, and try again; iteration and resilience as core entrepreneurial skills; famous failure-to-success stories
Let's practice together! Give an example of a famous entrepreneur who failed before succeeding
If you's first attempt at a project or business idea didn't work out, would they see it as a chance to improve rather than a reason to give up?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Learning from Failure. Give an example of a famous entrepreneur who failed before succeeding Explain what they would do differently if a business idea didn't work the first time
Today we're going to learn about Teamwork in Business. Get ready for an adventure!
Most businesses need more than one person; working together, dividing tasks, and using different people's strengths; the value of teamwork in business
Time to try this out: Describe how they would divide tasks in a team business project (one person makes, one sells, one designs)
If you was working with friends to run a stall at a fair, could they suggest how to divide up the jobs so everyone plays to their strengths?

Great job! Today we learned about Teamwork in Business. Describe how they would divide tasks in a team business project (one person makes, one sells, one designs) Explain why using people's different strengths makes a business better
Let's explore Having a Business Idea! Have you ever wondered about this?
Where business ideas come from; spotting everyday problems and thinking of solutions; the difference between inventing something new and improving something that exists
Time to try this out: Identify a simple everyday problem and suggest a product or service that could solve it
If you asked you to think of a problem at school or home that they could solve by making or selling something, could they come up with an idea?

Great job! Today we learned about Having a Business Idea. Identify a simple everyday problem and suggest a product or service that could solve it Explain the difference between an invention (something new) and an innovation (making something better)
Today we're going to learn about Being a Good Seller. Get ready for an adventure!
Customer service, fairness, and honesty in business; keeping promises to customers; why being trustworthy matters for repeat business and reputation
Let's practice together! Explain why being honest about what you're selling matters
If you sold something to a friend and it broke the next day, would they understand why it's important to make it right rather than ignore it?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Being a Good Seller. Explain why being honest about what you're selling matters Describe what good customer service looks like (being polite, helpful, keeping promises)
Let's explore Marketing Basics! Have you ever wondered about this?
How people find out about what you're selling; signs, posters, social media, word of mouth; being persuasive; the basics of advertising and promotion
Time to try this out: Design a simple poster or advert for a product or service
If you wanted to advertise a car wash or bake sale, could they design a poster that would make people want to come?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Marketing Basics. Design a simple poster or advert for a product or service Name at least three ways to let people know about something you're selling
